title: Incremental State Machines, Concurrency, Cancellation, and Ownership · Go Semantics Case: Cancellation Error Arbitration
summary: A parser is recursive and side-effectful, multiple goroutines read one connection, cancellation races with response writes, and error paths cannot state which component owns the buffer or close. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cancellation error arbitration increment in explicit protocol state, event, invariant, and ownership model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Arbitrate parse, application, peer, timeout, cancellation, write, and shutdown failures so exactly one terminal decision owns response and cleanup.
Assign one coordinated reader and ordered writer per HTTP/1.1 connection with explicit handoff for tunnels and shutdown.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Cancellation Error Arbitration · Theory Cancellation Error Arbitration
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 1435 in a partner TLS endpoint negotiating HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 through ALPN. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce cursor, framing, reader, and terminal ownership invariants; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then truncate one content octet and classify incomplete framing without connection reuse. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Arbitrate parse, application, peer, timeout, cancellation, write, and shutdown failures so exactly one terminal decision owns response and cleanup.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Cancellation Error Arbitration contract for the supplied data.
